name: Westport
description: Washington's surf capital. Jetty creates consistent sandbars. Cold, powerful, remote.

name: Seaside (The Point)
description: Oregon's most popular surf town. Protected right-hand point at the turnaround. Consistent and crowded.

name: Short Sands (Oswald West)
description: Oregon's gem, tucked inside Oswald West State Park. Sheltered cove, hike-in, old-growth vibe.

name: Pacific City (Cape Kiwanda)
description: Dramatic haystack rock backdrop. Dory fleet launches through the surf. Hollow beachbreak peaks.

name: Crescent City
description: Far NorCal beachbreak in the shadow of the redwoods. Cold, uncrowded, raw.

name: Mavericks
description: World-famous big wave break. Needs serious NW groundswell to light up. Reef bottom.

name: Ocean Beach
description: San Francisco's main break. Heavy, powerful beachbreak. Rips and fog are the vibe.

name: Fort Point
description: Wedging left under the Golden Gate Bridge. Iconic backdrop, needs solid NW groundswell.

name: Bolinas
description: Tucked-away NorCal gem. Locals keep the sign down for a reason. Sandbar peaks.

name: Pacifica
description: Linda Mar beach break just south of SF. More sheltered than OB. Good all-skill spot.

name: Steamer Lane
description: Santa Cruz's iconic point break. Consistent NW swell with kelp-padded rights.

name: Pleasure Point
description: Mellow right-hand reef steps down from 38th Ave to Privates. Perfect longboard wave.

name: Pismo Beach
description: Central Coast beach break between creamy sand dunes. Fun peaks, clam chowder after.

name: Rincon
description: The Queen of the Coast. Perfect right-hand point. Needs straight W or NW swell.

name: C Street (Ventura)
description: Ventura's long right-hand point. Mellow and user-friendly. Great longboard wave.

name: Malibu (Surfrider Beach)
description: The birthplace of California surf culture. Three points, noseride machines. Always crowded.

name: El Porto
description: Manhattan Beach's north end. LA's most consistent beachbreak. Works all year.

name: Huntington Beach Pier
description: Surf City USA. Reliable beachbreak both sides of the pier. US Open venue.

name: The Wedge
description: Newport's freak show shorebreak. Jetty reflection creates massive wedging peaks. Bodysurfer heaven.

name: Lower Trestles
description: World-class cobblestone reef. CT stop. Works on S swells in summer, NW in winter.

name: Grandview Beach
description: Encinitas reef at the north end of Leucadia. Staircase access, less crowded than Swamis.

name: Swamis
description: Encinitas reef point below the Self-Realization Fellowship. Long, walling rights.

name: Pipes (Encinitas)
description: Mid-Encinitas reef between Grandview and Swamis. Hollow, punchy peaks on the right swell.

name: Cardiff Reef
description: San Elijo reef break in Cardiff. Fun rights and lefts over cobblestone. Parking lot social scene.

name: Windansea
description: La Jolla's most classic reef. Notorious locals, thatched shack, excellent rights and lefts.

name: Blacks Beach
description: La Jolla's powerful reef. Hike-in. Best in long-period NW or WNW swells.

name: Pipeline
description: The most dangerous wave on earth. Phase 2 only — needs additional offshore buoy data.

name: Sunset Beach
description: North Shore's biggest consistent break. Unpredictable peaks across a vast reef. Triple crown venue.

name: Waimea Bay
description: The Bay only breaks over 20ft. When it does, the world watches. The Eddie is held here.

name: Honolua Bay
description: Maui's crown jewel. Deep-water right-hand point. Perfectly shaped walls when NW swells arrive.

name: Pe'ahi (Jaws)
description: Maui's tow-in big wave. Masts of water. Only breaks a handful of times per year.

name: Nantucket (Cisco Beach)
description: Exposed south-facing beach picks up more swell than anywhere in New England. Remote and wild.

name: Narragansett Town Beach
description: New England's most consistent break. Classic shore pound, fun beachbreak, gets good in fall.

name: Montauk (Ditch Plains)
description: The end of Long Island. Ditch Plains and beyond. Best in fall hurricane swell.

name: Rockaway Beach
description: NYC's surf break. Subway-accessible, surprisingly good in fall swells. Summer crowds ease off by September.

name: Manasquan Inlet
description: Jersey Shore's best break. Jetty-induced sandbars on both sides. Epic in NE groundswell.

name: Indian River Inlet
description: Delaware's best and only real surf spot. Jetty creates consistent peaks. Mid-Atlantic gem.

name: Ocean City (Inlet)
description: Maryland's surf town. Inlet jetty creates the best sandbars. Best in fall nor'easters.

name: Virginia Beach
description: Mid-Atlantic beachbreak. Best in fall with NE groundswells from nor'easters and hurricanes.

name: Kitty Hawk
description: Northern Outer Banks beach break. The Wright Brothers flew here for good reason — consistent wind.

name: Kill Devil Hills
description: Central Outer Banks. Multiple beach access points, fun peaks. Gets solid in hurricane season.

name: Nags Head Pier
description: OBX hub. Sandbars around Jennette's Pier. The most consistent peaks on the northern Banks.

name: Rodanthe (S-Turns)
description: Central OBX at the S-curves. Exposed to more swell than the northern Banks. Gets hollow.

name: Avon Pier
description: South of the S-turns. Pier creates sandbars. Locals know it as the most consistent OBX peak.

name: Cape Hatteras
description: Graveyard of the Atlantic. Fickle but can be epic. NE swells + W winds = fire.

name: Folly Beach
description: Charleston's beach break. Small and fun on a good day. Hurricane swell turns it on.

name: Tybee Island
description: Georgia's only real surf spot. Tiny by most standards, but locals love it. Hurricane swell required.

name: Jacksonville Beach
description: Northeast Florida's most consistent beach. Pier sandbars, warm water, active local scene.

name: Cocoa Beach
description: Space Coast surf. Ron Jon's backyard. Launches and landings visible from the lineup.

name: New Smyrna Beach
description: Florida's most consistent beachbreak. Fun peaks, warm water, and the shark capital of the world.

name: Sebastian Inlet
description: Florida's best wave. Jetty-assisted hollow right. Lights up during NE swells.

name: Jeffreys Bay
description: Arguably the world's best right-hand point. Supertubes to Impossibles. CT stop.

name: Hossegor (La Gravière)
description: Europe's Pipeline. Deep sandbar funnels powerful Atlantic swells into thick, barreling peaks.

name: Uluwatu
description: Bali's crown jewel. Long left-hand reef below cliff temples. Works year-round.

name: Puerto Escondido
description: The Mexican Pipeline. Terrifying shorebreak on a steep sandbar. Can reach 30ft in summer.

name: Pavones
description: One of the longest left-hand point breaks on earth. Ride all the way to the river mouth.

name: Snapper Rocks
description: Start of the Superbank. Famed right-hander with long cobblestone point runs to Kirra.

name: Taghazout (Anchor Point)
description: Morocco's best right-hand point. Long walls, argan oil, cheap tagine. Europe's winter escape.

name: Cloudbreak
description: Boat-access left-hand reef in the middle of the Pacific. Pristine barrels on a razor-sharp reef.

name: Sunset Cliffs
description: San Diego's dramatic reef-lined cliffs. Multiple spots from Garbage Beach to Abs. Swell-magnets.

name: Ocean Beach Pier (SD)
description: San Diego's OB pier peaks. Consistent beach break, mellow crowd, easy parking.

name: Pacific Beach Shore
description: PB's boardwalk beach. Mellow, social scene. Good learner wave in summer S swells.

name: Mission Beach
description: Classic San Diego beach town break. Fun summer peaks, crowded on weekends.

name: Oceanside Harbor
description: North San Diego County jetty break. Harbor mouth sandbars produce hollow rights.

name: San Onofre State Beach
description: Old Man's, Dog Patch, and Trail 1. Classic longboard hang-ten vibes below the nuclear plant.

name: Salt Creek
description: Dana Point's best reef. Consistent right-hand point, excellent in S swells. Ritz Carlton backdrop.

name: Brooks Street
description: Laguna Beach reef. Mellow right-hander, good longboard wave. Classic SoCal scene.

name: 54th Street Newport
description: Newport Beach mid-peninsula. Fun beachbreak peak, less crowded than the Pier.

name: Seal Beach Pier
description: OC's northernmost pier break. Small but fun. Locals-only vibe in a sleepy beach town.

name: Redondo Breakwall
description: Breakwall-induced peaks in the South Bay. Fun left-handers on SSW swells.

name: Venice Beach
description: LA's iconic beach. Muscle Beach, boardwalk circus, and occasional fun surf. People-watching paradise.

name: Zuma Beach
description: Malibu's big beach. Multiple peaks across a long stretch. Works on bigger NW swells.

name: Leo Carrillo State Park
description: Ventura County line point break. Rock reef and sand combo. Fun right-handers camping adjacent.

name: Emma Wood
description: Ventura rivermouth beachbreak. Sandbars shift with river discharge. Consistent and fun.

name: Poche Beach
description: San Clemente beachbreak north of Trestles. Low-key peaks, easy access, consistent in summer S swells.

name: Avila Beach
description: Protected Central Coast cove. Mellow waves, warm microclimate, pier-to-pier peaks.

name: Morro Bay
description: Central Coast beachbreak in the shadow of Morro Rock. Consistent NW swell, cold water, sea otters.

name: Backdoor Pipeline
description: Pipeline's right-hand sibling. Spits harder, breaks shallower. Same reef, opposite direction.

name: Off the Wall
description: Shallow reef directly in front of the famous house. Powerful, hollow right-handers.

name: Rocky Point
description: Fun North Shore reef between Pipe and Sunset. Rights and lefts on mixed reef/sand.

name: Velzyland
description: East end North Shore. Punchy, hollow right-handers. Less crowded than the main breaks.

name: Laniakea
description: Turtle Beach point. Long right-hand walls, sea turtles in the lineup. Popular but spread out.

name: Haleiwa
description: Ali'i Beach Park reef. Triple Crown venue. Rights and lefts over varied reef sections.

name: Ala Moana Bowls
description: Honolulu's best reef. Perfect A-frame bowls on summer S swell. Classic shortboard wave.

name: Diamond Head
description: Right-hander below the crater. Long walls on S swell, excellent for intermediate surfers.

name: Sandy Beach
description: Oahu's boneyard. Powerful shorebreak snaps spines. Watch the locals before you paddle out.

name: Makaha
description: West side big wave. Home of the Makaha International. Huge, grinding rights on W swell.

name: Hookipa
description: Maui's windsurfing and surfing mecca. Powerful reef peaks. Sea turtles everywhere.

name: Dumps (Maalaea)
description: Said to be the world's fastest wave. Short, insane right-hander through Maalaea harbor.

name: La Perouse Bay
description: South Maui lava field reef. Remote, pristine, powerful. Hike or drive the lava road.

name: Banyans
description: Big Island's best reef. Long rights in Kona. Named for the banyan trees on the seawall.

name: Pine Trees
description: Kona coast reef with casuarina trees. Playful peaks, local favorite, accessible.

name: Hanalei Bay
description: Kauai's north shore bay. Multiple breaks across a stunning valley backdrop. Winter gets big.

name: Tunnels Beach
description: Makua reef, aka Tunnels. Outer reef breaks big in winter. Stunning Na Pali coast views.

name: Pakala (Infinities)
description: Kauai's endless left. Rides of 500m on a good day. Walk the tracks in to find it.

name: Ruggles
description: Rhode Island's heaving shore break reef. Dangerous, consequential, and the best RI has to offer.

name: Charlestown Breachway
description: RI jetty break. Current-assisted peaks beside the breachway. Fun with W winds.

name: Bay Head
description: South Jersey's best beachbreak town. Locals-only attitude, but worth the trip on NE groundswell.

name: Belmar 5th Ave
description: Jersey Shore staple. Consistent beachbreak, big surf scene, summer contests.

name: Asbury Park
description: Springsteen country. Fun peaks north and south of the Stone Pony. Revived beach town energy.

name: Ponce Inlet
description: Inlet jetty south of New Smyrna. Sandbars both sides, concentrated swell energy.

name: K38 (Km 38)
description: Baja's most accessible point break. Right-hander over reef/sand. Good all skill levels.

name: Islas de Todos Santos
description: Mexico's Mavericks. Boat-access island 20 miles south of Ensenada. Giant right-handers.

name: San Miguel
description: Ensenada's home break. Right-hand point break with cobblestone reef. Classic Baja.

name: Sayulita
description: Hippie-chic Pacific Mexico town. Right-hand point plus beach peaks. Learn-to-surf capital.

name: Punta de Mita
description: Riviera Nayarit point break. La Lancha and The Cove — excellent rights on SW swell.

name: Nexpa
description: Mexican point break perfection. Long lefts off a rivermouth sandbar. Completely uncrowded.

name: Barra de la Cruz
description: WCT-quality right-hand point. Perfect walls on big S swell. Village life, no frills.

name: Tamarindo
description: Costa Rica's most developed surf town. Multiple beach peaks, surf schools everywhere.

name: Nosara (Playa Guiones)
description: Pristine CR beach break. Yoga and surf capital. Consistent, fun peaks in a wildlife refuge.

name: Dominical
description: South Costa Rica beach break. Powerful and consistent. Jungle meets ocean.

name: Salsa Brava
description: Caribbean Costa Rica reef. Hollow right-handers over a sharp coral shelf. Caribbean soul.

name: Punta Roca
description: El Salvador's best right-hand point. Long rides over reef, strong rip current, serious locals.

name: El Zonte
description: Bitcoin Beach. Volcanic sand, consistent beach break, chill community vibe.

name: Chicama
description: Longest left-hand wave in the world. Rides of over 4km on a perfect day. Pure magic.

name: Pico Alto
description: Peru's big wave. Outer reef peaks over 30ft. Rarely surfed, extremely powerful.

name: Huanchaco
description: Ancient Peruvian fishing village, birthplace of the caballito de totora reed craft. Fun left point.

name: Itacare
description: Bahia's gem. Multiple beaches accessible by trail through Atlantic rainforest. Tiririca is classic.

name: Maresias
description: São Paulo state's best beach break. Good barreling beach peaks. Busy summer scene.

name: Joaquina
description: Florianopolis's south beach. Consistent S swell, hollow peaks, national contest venue.

name: Cote des Basques
description: Biarritz's legendary beach below the Grand Plage. Birthplace of European surfing.

name: Supertubos
description: Peniche's powerhouse. European Pipeline. Thick, hollow barrels on a sandbar peak. CT stop.

name: Ribeira d'Ilhas
description: Ericeira's crown jewel right-hand reef. World Surfing Reserve. Long walling rights.

name: Mundaka
description: Europe's best left-hand barrel. Sandbars shift with the Mundaka estuary. World-class when on.

name: Padang Padang
description: Impossibly perfect left-hand cave tube. Low tide drains the reef for maximum barrel.

name: Kuta Beach
description: Bali's original tourist beach. Mellow beachbreak, great for beginners, endless warung food.

name: Echo Beach (Canggu)
description: Canggu's trendy reef break. Good left and right, lively cafe scene on the cliff.

name: Keramas
description: East Bali's best right-hand reef. Night surfing lights, pro CT venue, powerful barrels.

name: Desert Point (Bangko Bangko)
description: The perfect left-hander at the end of the earth. Long barrel rides on a razor-sharp reef.

name: HT's (Hollow Trees)
description: Mentawai's crown jewel. Perfect left-hand barrels in a remote tropical paradise. Boat access only.

name: Macaronis
description: The perfect fun left. Long, groomed walls in the Mentawais. Accessible for intermediates.

name: Kirra
description: End of the Superbank. When the sand lines up right, the longest barrels in Australia.

name: Burleigh Heads
description: Perfect point break wrapping around headland. Can barrel on low tide. CT venue.

name: Noosa Heads
description: Queensland longboard mecca. Tea Tree, First Point, National Park. Pristine and mellow.

name: Narrabeen
description: Sydney's second city surf spot. Long beachbreak, strong local scene, CT history.

name: Manly Beach
description: Sydney's icon. Ferry access from the CBD. Fun peaks, surf culture history, annual festival.

name: Bells Beach
description: Australia's most iconic surf break. Easter Rip Curl Pro has run here since 1961. Heavy rights.

name: Winki Pop
description: Right-hand reef just around the corner from Bells. Shorter but punchier and more hollow.

name: Surfers Point (Margaret River)
description: WA's world-class left-hand reef. Raw Southern Ocean power. CT stop.

name: Yallingup
description: Cape Naturaliste reef. Various breaks from Main Break to Three Bears. SW Indian Ocean swell.

name: École de Surf de Bretagne (La Torche)
Escola de Surf Paz na Terra
Ellis Beach Surf Life Saving Club
Windsurfing und Segelclub Wetter 76 e.V. Bernd Barenberg
Stalls for Renting Surf Boards / Lessons
Agnes Water Surf Life Saving Club
Palm Beach Tenerife Surf Shop and School
Blue Ocean Ericeira Surf School & Rentals
Surf'Lab Location de vélo et surf
École de Surf Côte Française Vieux-Boucau
Spot de Surf Cantinho da Bahía
Club de Stand Up Paddle & Surf de Nyon
Punta Sur
region: Archipielago de San Andres, Providencia y Santa Catalina

name: La Roca
region: Archipielago de San Andres, Providencia y Santa Catalina

name: 釣ヶ崎海岸サーフィンビーチ
École de Surf de Bretagne de Penhors
Spor de surf la grève blanche
Amici del mare surf and skate school
Ecole de surf Atlantic Surf Lodge
Vibe Surf School Sunny Isles Beach
木崎浜サーフポイント
Take Off Personal Surftraining Meeting Point
Apollo Bay Surf Life Saving Club
Zone de Surf du Grand Crohot
Zone de surf du Truc Vert
Board'n Breakfast Surf House Chambre d'hôtes
Atlantic Surf lodge / Ecole de surf
École de Surf de Bretagne - Audierne
Centro de Alto Rendimento de Surf de Viana do Castelo
Reparación de Tablas para Surfear Dr. Ding
Plage du Moulin de la Rive
Centro de Alto Rendimento de Surf da Nazaré
片瀬西浜・鵠沼海水浴場
Centro de Alto Rendimiento de Surf
Jamal Surf - surf rental and lessons
Surf School ASSP (association de surf skhirat plage)
Centro de Alto Rendimento de Surf de São Jacinto
